food.feeding_frequency: 2 meals per day for adults, 3–4 meals for puppies
food.portion_tip: Follow package guidelines based on weight; adjust for activity level
food.special_notes: Always provide fresh water. Consult your vet for breed-specific dietary needs.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: First dose at 12–16 weeks, booster at 1 year, then every 1–3 years

core_vaccines.name: Distemper (DHPP)
core_vaccines.schedule: 3 doses at 6–8, 10–12, and 14–16 weeks; booster at 1 year, then every 3 years

optional_vaccines.name: Bordetella (Kennel Cough)
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended if dog visits boarding, grooming, or dog parks

optional_vaccines.name: Leptospirosis
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended in areas with wildlife or standing water

optional_vaccines.name: Canine Influenza
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended for social dogs in outbreak areas

vaccines.deworming: Deworm puppies at 2, 4, 6, 8 weeks; adults every 3–6 months
vaccines.special_notes: Follow your veterinarian's recommended schedule. Keep vaccination records updated.

behavior.temperament: Varies by breed — loyal, friendly, and intelligent
behavior.trainability: Moderate — responds well to positive reinforcement
behavior.social_traits: Generally good with families; early socialization recommended
behavior.exercise_needs: 30–60 minutes of daily exercise
behavior.good_with_children: Yes, with proper socialization
behavior.special_notes: Every dog is unique. Temperament can vary based on upbringing and training.

recommended_foods: Fish oil supplements for coat health
food.portion_tip: Monitor weight closely — Golden Retrievers are prone to obesity
food.special_notes: Omega-3 fatty acids help maintain their luxurious coat. Consider joint supplements for older dogs.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: 12–16 weeks, booster at 1 year, then every 3 years

optional_vaccines.name: Bordetella
optional_vaccines.when: Highly recommended — Goldens are social dogs

optional_vaccines.name: Leptospirosis
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended — Goldens love water and outdoor activities

vaccines.deworming: Every 3 months for active outdoor dogs
vaccines.special_notes: Goldens are prone to cancer — regular vet checkups are essential.

behavior.temperament: Friendly, reliable, and trustworthy. One of the most beloved family dogs.
behavior.trainability: Excellent — eager to please, very intelligent (ranked #4 in intelligence)
behavior.social_traits: Extremely social, loves everyone including strangers. Great therapy dogs.
behavior.exercise_needs: 60–90 minutes daily — loves swimming, fetching, and hiking
behavior.barking_tendency: Moderate — barks to alert but not excessive
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — patient and gentle with kids
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Excellent — very friendly with other animals
behavior.special_notes: Prone to separation anxiety if left alone too long. They thrive on human companionship.

food.portion_tip: Labs are notorious overeaters — use measured portions and avoid free-feeding
food.special_notes: Labs have a genetic mutation that makes them always feel hungry. Weight management is critical.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: 12–16 weeks, booster at 1 year, then every 3 years

vaccines.special_notes: Labs are prone to hip dysplasia — regular vet monitoring recommended.

behavior.temperament: Outgoing, active, and friendly. America's most popular breed for decades.
behavior.trainability: Excellent — highly intelligent and food-motivated
behavior.social_traits: Extremely friendly with everyone. Natural service and guide dogs.
behavior.exercise_needs: 60–120 minutes daily — loves swimming, retrieving, and running
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — gentle, patient, and playful
behavior.special_notes: Labs need mental stimulation too — puzzle toys and training sessions prevent destructive behavior.

food.portion_tip: Feed for ideal weight, not current weight. GSDs can have sensitive stomachs.
food.special_notes: German Shepherds are prone to bloat (GDV) — avoid exercise 1 hour before/after meals.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: 12–16 weeks, booster at 1 year, then every 3 years

core_vaccines.name: Parvovirus
core_vaccines.schedule: Extra dose recommended — GSDs are more susceptible

vaccines.deworming: Every 3 months, more frequently for working dogs
vaccines.special_notes: GSDs are prone to degenerative myelopathy — discuss genetic testing with your vet.

behavior.temperament: Confident, courageous, and smart. World's premier working dog.
behavior.trainability: Exceptional — ranked #3 in intelligence, eager to work
behavior.social_traits: Loyal and protective of family. Can be aloof with strangers.
behavior.exercise_needs: 90–120 minutes daily — needs both physical and mental challenges
behavior.good_with_children: Very good — protective and gentle with family children
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Good with proper socialization from puppyhood
behavior.special_notes: Require a confident owner. Without proper training, they can become anxious or overprotective.

food.portion_tip: Bulldogs gain weight very easily — strict portion control is essential
food.special_notes: Bulldogs are prone to food allergies. Consider a hypoallergenic diet if skin issues arise.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: 12–16 weeks, booster at 1 year, then every 3 years

optional_vaccines.name: Bordetella
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended due to brachycephalic respiratory sensitivity

optional_vaccines.name: Canine Influenza
optional_vaccines.when: Important — respiratory infections are more serious for flat-faced breeds

vaccines.special_notes: Brachycephalic breed — monitor closely for respiratory issues after vaccination.

behavior.temperament: Calm, courageous, and friendly. A gentle companion.
behavior.trainability: Moderate — can be stubborn, responds to patience and treats
behavior.social_traits: Very affectionate, loves being around people. Good apartment dogs.
behavior.exercise_needs: 20–30 minutes daily — short walks, avoid heat and overexertion
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — patient and sturdy
behavior.special_notes: Cannot tolerate heat or intense exercise due to brachycephalic anatomy. Keep cool!

food.portion_tip: Beagles are food-obsessed — never free-feed, measure every meal
food.special_notes: Beagles will eat anything they find. Keep food secured and trash bins closed.

optional_vaccines.name: Leptospirosis
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended — Beagles love sniffing everything outdoors

vaccines.special_notes: Beagles are generally healthy but prone to ear infections — keep ears clean and dry.

behavior.temperament: Merry, friendly, and curious. A classic scent hound.
behavior.trainability: Moderate — intelligent but easily distracted by scents
behavior.social_traits: Pack dogs — get along great with other dogs and people
behavior.exercise_needs: 60–90 minutes daily — loves walks, sniffing adventures, and playtime
behavior.barking_tendency: High — known for howling and baying
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — playful and sturdy
behavior.special_notes: Beagles follow their nose everywhere. Always use a leash outdoors — they will chase scents.

food.diet_type: High-quality protein with omega fatty acids
food.feeding_frequency: 2 meals per day (portion varies by size: toy, miniature, standard)
food.portion_tip: Adjust portions based on Poodle size variant — Toy: 0.5 cup, Mini: 1 cup, Standard: 2 cups
food.special_notes: Poodles benefit greatly from fish oil supplements for their iconic curly coat.

optional_vaccines.name: Bordetella
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended for grooming and show dogs

vaccines.special_notes: Poodles are prone to Addison's disease — regular blood work recommended.

behavior.temperament: Proud, intelligent, and elegant. Highly versatile companion.
behavior.trainability: Exceptional — ranked #2 in intelligence among all breeds
behavior.social_traits: Bonds closely with family. Can be reserved with strangers initially.
behavior.exercise_needs: 60+ minutes daily — loves swimming, agility, and retrieving
behavior.good_with_children: Very good — gentle and patient
behavior.special_notes: Hypoallergenic coat requires regular grooming every 4–6 weeks. Mental stimulation is as important as physical.

food.diet_type: High-protein, moderate-fat diet for muscle maintenance
food.portion_tip: Feed for lean body condition — Rotties should have a visible waist
food.special_notes: Prone to bloat — use slow-feeder bowls and avoid exercise around meal times.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: 12–16 weeks, booster at 1 year, then every 3 years

core_vaccines.name: Parvovirus
core_vaccines.schedule: Rottweilers are high-risk — extra booster at 18 weeks recommended

vaccines.special_notes: Rottweilers are more susceptible to Parvovirus — ensure complete vaccination series.

behavior.temperament: Loyal, confident, and protective. A powerful guardian breed.
behavior.trainability: Very good — intelligent and eager to work, but needs firm leadership
behavior.social_traits: Devoted to family, naturally protective. Reserved with strangers.
behavior.exercise_needs: 60–90 minutes daily — enjoys walks, swimming, and obedience work
behavior.barking_tendency: Low to moderate — barks with purpose
behavior.good_with_children: Good with family children when raised together
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Can be dominant — early socialization is key
behavior.special_notes: Require experienced owners. Early socialization and consistent training are essential.

food.feeding_frequency: 3–4 small meals per day (Yorkies are prone to hypoglycemia)
food.portion_tip: 0.25–0.5 cup per day total — tiny dogs need tiny portions
food.special_notes: Yorkies have sensitive stomachs and are prone to low blood sugar. Never skip meals.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: Standard schedule — monitor for vaccine reactions due to small size

vaccines.special_notes: Small breeds can have more vaccine reactions — discuss spacing vaccinations with your vet.

behavior.temperament: Bold, confident, and affectionate. Big personality in a tiny body.
behavior.trainability: Moderate — intelligent but can be stubborn. Positive reinforcement works best.
behavior.social_traits: Bonds deeply with owner. Can be feisty with larger dogs.
behavior.exercise_needs: 30–45 minutes daily — short walks and indoor play
behavior.good_with_children: Better with older children — too fragile for rough play
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Can be bossy with other dogs, generally okay with cats
behavior.special_notes: Yorkies are fragile — handle with care. Dental disease is very common — brush teeth regularly.

food.portion_tip: Boxers should be lean and muscular — avoid overfeeding
food.special_notes: Boxers are prone to heart issues — ensure diet includes taurine and L-carnitine.

vaccines.special_notes: Boxers are cancer-prone — regular vet checkups and early screening recommended.

behavior.temperament: Fun-loving, bright, and energetic. The clown of the dog world.
behavior.trainability: Good — intelligent but can be goofy and easily distracted
behavior.social_traits: Extremely affectionate, great family dog. Protective but friendly.
behavior.exercise_needs: 60–90 minutes daily — loves play sessions, running, and agility
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — playful, patient, and protective
behavior.special_notes: Boxers are eternal puppies — they stay playful well into adulthood. Cannot tolerate extreme heat.

food.feeding_frequency: 2 meals per day (0.5–1.5 cups depending on size)
food.portion_tip: Weight control is CRITICAL — extra weight causes back injuries in Dachshunds
food.special_notes: Even 1–2 extra pounds on a Dachshund greatly increases risk of IVDD (back disease).

optional_vaccines.name: Lyme Disease
optional_vaccines.when: In tick-prone areas — low-to-ground dogs pick up more ticks

vaccines.special_notes: Monitor for back issues (IVDD) — avoid jumping on/off furniture.

behavior.temperament: Clever, lively, and courageous. Surprisingly brave for their size.
behavior.trainability: Moderate — intelligent but independent and stubborn
behavior.social_traits: Loyal and attached to family. Can be wary of strangers.
behavior.exercise_needs: 30–60 minutes daily — walks and play, avoid jumping and stairs
behavior.good_with_children: Good with older children who handle them gently
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Can be territorial — early socialization helps
behavior.special_notes: NEVER let them jump off furniture or go down stairs unsupported — back injury risk is very high.

food.portion_tip: Huskies eat surprisingly little for their size — they're metabolically efficient
food.special_notes: Huskies may refuse food when not hungry — this is normal, not a cause for concern.

vaccines.special_notes: Huskies are generally very healthy — one of the healthiest purebreds.

behavior.temperament: Outgoing, mischievous, and free-spirited. Born to run.
behavior.trainability: Challenging — very intelligent but independent and stubborn
behavior.social_traits: Friendly with everyone including strangers. Poor guard dogs!
behavior.exercise_needs: 90–120+ minutes daily — running, hiking, pulling activities
behavior.barking_tendency: Moderate barking but VERY vocal — known for howling and 'talking'
behavior.good_with_children: Very good — gentle and playful
behavior.good_with_other_pets: High prey drive — supervise with small animals
behavior.special_notes: Notorious escape artists — need secure fencing (6ft minimum). Not suited for hot climates.

food.portion_tip: Small meals prevent bloating in this small breed
food.special_notes: Shih Tzus can be picky eaters. Dental issues are common — consider dental-care kibble.

vaccines.special_notes: Brachycephalic breed — keep airways clear and monitor breathing.

behavior.temperament: Affectionate, happy, and outgoing. A true companion breed.
behavior.trainability: Moderate — charming but can be stubborn about housetraining
behavior.social_traits: Loves being the center of attention. Good with everyone.
behavior.exercise_needs: 20–30 minutes daily — short walks and indoor play
behavior.good_with_children: Good — gentle but better with older gentle children
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Very good — friendly with other animals
behavior.special_notes: Coat requires daily brushing or a short 'puppy cut'. Prone to eye issues — keep face clean.

food.portion_tip: Dobes need lean muscle — avoid overfeeding carbs
food.special_notes: Dobermans are prone to DCM (heart disease) — taurine supplementation is critical.

core_vaccines.name: Parvovirus
core_vaccines.schedule: Dobermans are high-risk — extra booster recommended

vaccines.special_notes: Dobermans are Parvovirus-susceptible — do not skip boosters. Screen for DCM annually.

behavior.temperament: Loyal, fearless, and alert. One of the best personal protection dogs.
behavior.trainability: Excellent — ranked #5 in intelligence, loves to work
behavior.social_traits: Velcro dogs — deeply bonded with their person. Naturally protective.
behavior.exercise_needs: 60–90 minutes daily — running, hiking, agility training
behavior.barking_tendency: Moderate — barks with purpose as an alert dog
behavior.good_with_children: Very good with family children
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Can be dominant — socialization is important
behavior.special_notes: Need to be with their people — suffer greatly from isolation. Best for active, involved owners.

food.feeding_frequency: 2–3 meals per day (3–5 cups per meal for adults)
food.portion_tip: Use elevated feeding bowls and slow feeders to prevent bloat
food.special_notes: CRITICAL: Use large-breed puppy food — standard puppy food causes bone growth problems.

vaccines.special_notes: Great Danes have shorter lifespans (7–10 years) — proactive health care is vital.

behavior.temperament: Friendly, patient, and dependable. The 'gentle giant' of dogs.
behavior.trainability: Good — eager to please despite their size
behavior.social_traits: Affectionate and loves to lean on people. Surprisingly good apartment dogs.
behavior.exercise_needs: 45–60 minutes daily — moderate walks, avoid intense exercise during growth
behavior.barking_tendency: Low to moderate — deep bark is intimidating enough
behavior.good_with_children: Very good — gentle despite size (supervise due to size)
behavior.special_notes: Bloat (GDV) is the #1 killer of Great Danes — learn the signs and consider preventive surgery.

food.feeding_frequency: 3–4 small meals daily (Chihuahuas are prone to hypoglycemia)
food.portion_tip: Only 0.25–0.5 cup per day — measure carefully, even small overfeeding causes obesity
food.special_notes: Always have food available for young Chihuahuas — hypoglycemia can be fatal in tiny puppies.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: Standard — some vets recommend spacing vaccines due to tiny size

core_vaccines.name: DHPP
core_vaccines.schedule: Standard puppy series — some vets split into individual vaccines

optional_vaccines.name: Canine Influenza
optional_vaccines.when: Respiratory infections hit small breeds harder

vaccines.deworming: Every 3–6 months — precise weight-based dosing essential
vaccines.special_notes: Tiny breeds can have vaccine reactions — discuss spacing vaccines with your vet.

behavior.temperament: Charming, graceful, and sassy. Fiercely loyal to one person.
behavior.trainability: Moderate — clever but can be willful. Consistency is key.
behavior.social_traits: Bonds intensely with owner. Can be snappy with strangers if not socialized.
behavior.exercise_needs: 20–30 minutes daily — short walks and indoor play
behavior.barking_tendency: Very High — one of the most vocal breeds
behavior.good_with_children: Better with older gentle children — too fragile for toddlers
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Often prefers other Chihuahuas; can be bossy with bigger dogs
behavior.special_notes: Fragile bones — handle carefully. Keep warm in cold weather. Dental care is essential.

food.portion_tip: 0.25–0.5 cup per day total — Poms gain weight easily under all that fluff
food.special_notes: Dense coat hides weight gain — weigh regularly at the vet.

optional_vaccines.name: Bordetella
optional_vaccines.when: Recommended for grooming and social outings

vaccines.special_notes: Poms are prone to collapsed trachea — use harnesses instead of collars.

behavior.temperament: Lively, bold, and curious. Spirited extrovert.
behavior.trainability: Good — smart and loves performing tricks
behavior.social_traits: Confident and outgoing. May try to boss around larger dogs.
behavior.exercise_needs: 30–45 minutes daily — walks and play sessions
behavior.good_with_children: Better with older children due to small size
behavior.special_notes: Double coat needs regular brushing (3x/week minimum). Never shave a Pom's coat.

foods_to_avoid: Soy, wheat, corn (common allergens for Frenchies)
food.portion_tip: Frenchies gain weight fast — maintain strict portion control
food.special_notes: French Bulldogs are allergy-prone — an elimination diet may be needed to find safe foods.

vaccines.special_notes: Brachycephalic breed — respiratory infections are more dangerous. Prioritize respiratory vaccines.

behavior.temperament: Adaptable, playful, and smart. The perfect city companion.
behavior.trainability: Moderate — smart but can be stubborn. Food motivation helps.
behavior.social_traits: Very sociable, loves people and other dogs. Minimal guarding instinct.
behavior.exercise_needs: 30–45 minutes daily — short walks, avoid heat and overexertion
behavior.barking_tendency: Low — one of the quieter breeds
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — sturdy and playful
behavior.special_notes: Cannot swim (heavy front). Cannot tolerate heat. Snoring is completely normal!

food.portion_tip: Active Border Collies may need more; adjust based on activity level
food.special_notes: Working Border Collies may need up to 30% more calories than pet Collies.

vaccines.deworming: Every 3 months — more frequently for farm dogs
vaccines.special_notes: IMPORTANT: Border Collies may carry the MDR1 gene — some drugs (like ivermectin) can be toxic. Get tested.

behavior.temperament: Brilliant, energetic, and driven. The smartest of all dog breeds.
behavior.trainability: Unmatched — #1 most intelligent breed. Learns commands in under 5 repetitions.
behavior.social_traits: Bonds deeply with handler. May try to 'herd' children and other pets.
behavior.exercise_needs: 120+ minutes daily — needs a JOB to do (herding, agility, flyball)
behavior.barking_tendency: Moderate to High — especially when stimulated
behavior.good_with_children: Good — but may nip at heels (herding instinct)
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Good but may try to herd them
behavior.special_notes: NOT a couch potato breed. Under-stimulated Border Collies become destructive. They need mental AND physical work.

food.diet_type: Moderate-protein food with ear and skin health support
food.portion_tip: Cocker Spaniels love food and gain weight easily
food.special_notes: Prone to ear infections — diet with omega-3 and anti-inflammatory ingredients helps.

optional_vaccines.name: Lyme Disease
optional_vaccines.when: In tick areas — long ears drag in brush

vaccines.special_notes: Clean ears weekly — Cockers are extremely prone to ear infections.

behavior.temperament: Gentle, happy, and trusting. The 'merry Cocker'.
behavior.trainability: Very good — eager to please and responsive
behavior.social_traits: Loves everyone — one of the friendliest breeds
behavior.exercise_needs: 45–60 minutes daily — walks, fetch, and swimming
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — gentle and patient
behavior.special_notes: Regular grooming needed. Prone to 'submissive urination' — use positive-only training methods.

food.portion_tip: Adjust portions based on activity — working Aussies need more fuel
food.special_notes: Like Border Collies, Aussies may carry the MDR1 gene. Get tested before deworming.

vaccines.special_notes: MDR1 gene sensitivity — some common medications can be dangerous. Always inform your vet.

behavior.temperament: Smart, work-oriented, and exuberant. A herding powerhouse.
behavior.trainability: Excellent — highly intelligent and eager to learn
behavior.social_traits: Loyal to family, can be reserved with strangers. Strong herding instinct.
behavior.exercise_needs: 90–120 minutes daily — agility, frisbee, hiking, herding
behavior.good_with_children: Very good — may try to herd kids
behavior.special_notes: Will herd everything — kids, cats, other dogs. Need a job or activity to stay happy.

foods_to_avoid: Foods with red dye (causes tear staining)
food.portion_tip: 0.25–0.5 cup daily — tiny breed needs tiny portions
food.special_notes: Feed foods without red dye or beet pulp to reduce tear staining.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: Standard schedule — may space for small size

vaccines.special_notes: Small breeds may benefit from spaced-out vaccination schedule.

behavior.temperament: Gentle, playful, and charming. A classic lap dog with spirit.
behavior.trainability: Good — responsive to gentle training and praise
behavior.social_traits: Loves attention, bonds closely with family. Can develop separation anxiety.
behavior.exercise_needs: 20–30 minutes daily — short walks, indoor play
behavior.good_with_children: Better with older gentle children
behavior.special_notes: Coat needs daily brushing. Tear staining requires daily cleaning. Dental care is critical.

food.feeding_frequency: 2–3 meals per day (4–6 cups total for adults)
food.portion_tip: Split meals to prevent bloat — never feed one large meal
food.special_notes: High risk of bloat (GDV). Use elevated slow-feeder bowls. Rest after meals.

vaccines.special_notes: Giant breeds need careful growth management — never rush growth with supplements.

behavior.temperament: Gentle, patient, and watchful. The ultimate gentle giant.
behavior.trainability: Good — eager to please but can be slow/stubborn
behavior.social_traits: Wonderful with everyone. Natural nanny dog instinct.
behavior.exercise_needs: 30–60 minutes daily — moderate walks, avoid overexertion especially in heat
behavior.good_with_children: Exceptional — incredibly gentle and patient
behavior.special_notes: Drool is unavoidable! Cannot tolerate heat well. Short lifespan (8–10 years).

food.portion_tip: Feed for a lean, muscular build — you should see a defined waist
food.special_notes: Pit Bulls are prone to skin allergies — limited-ingredient diets often work best.

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.schedule: Standard schedule — required by law in most areas

core_vaccines.name: Parvovirus
core_vaccines.schedule: Pit Bulls can be more susceptible — complete all boosters

vaccines.special_notes: Keep vaccination records — some areas have breed-specific regulations.

behavior.temperament: Loyal, affectionate, and eager to please. Often called 'nanny dogs'.
behavior.trainability: Very good — intelligent and people-focused
behavior.social_traits: Extremely affectionate with family. Dog-selectiveness is common.
behavior.exercise_needs: 60–90 minutes daily — loves tug, fetch, and hiking
behavior.good_with_children: Excellent — known for being gentle and patient with kids
behavior.good_with_other_pets: Varies — early socialization is important. May be dog-selective.
behavior.special_notes: Training and socialization are essential. They aim to please — positive reinforcement gets amazing results.
